title: TDS on SiteFlow Subscription Payment — Section 194J Guide
metaTitle: TDS on SiteFlow Subscription Payment — Section 194J Guide
metaDescription: TDS on SiteFlow subscription payments is 2% under Section 194J. Covers why it is 2% and not 10%, when TDS applies, and how to issue Form 16A to SiteFlow.
author: SiteFlow Operations Team
body:
Quick Answer

TDS on SiteFlow subscription payments: 2%

SiteFlow is a software product and technical service. Under Section 194J of the Income Tax Act, TDS on technical services (including software products and services) is 2% effective from FY 2020-21.

Why 2% and Not 10%?

Section 194J covers TDS on fees for Professional and Technical Services:

SiteFlow is classified as a software product and technical service. The applicable rate is therefore 2%, not 10%. This rate change from 10% to 2% for technical services was introduced from FY 2020-21 onwards.

When Does TDS Apply?

TDS under Section 194J applies when both of the following are true:

  • The payer is a company, or an individual or HUF liable to get accounts audited
  • The total payment to a single party exceeds Rs 30,000 in a financial year

If your organisation meets both criteria, you are required to deduct 2% TDS on your SiteFlow subscription payment before remitting it.

What to Do After Deducting TDS

  1. Deduct 2% TDS at the time of payment or credit, whichever is earlier
  2. Deposit the TDS to the government using your TAN number
  3. Issue Form 16A (TDS certificate) to Scroll Construction Pvt. Ltd. for the amount deducted
  4. Share the Form 16A details with the SiteFlow support team so the amount can be reconciled against your invoice

Tips

  • Your finance or accounts team handles the TDS deduction and deposit — this is a tax compliance step and is not done inside the SiteFlow app
  • If you are unsure whether TDS applies to your organisation, consult your CA or finance team
  • Always issue Form 16A to SiteFlow after deducting — without it, the TDS amount cannot be claimed on SiteFlow’s side

If Something Goes Wrong
